material_overrides: Post-term non-compete may not be enforceable under CA law
General release for successor/transfer may be unenforceable (Corp Code 31512, B&P Code 20010)
NH governing law may not be enforceable
Binding arbitration in Portsmouth NH may be subject to CA procedural rules (B&P Code 20040.5, CCP 1281, Federal Arbitration Act)
Waiver of punitive damages may not be enforceable
Material modification requires DFPI filing with existing/proposed terms disclosure
No waiver of claims under state franchise law or disclaimer of reliance
Max interest rate: 10%
Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs
Sections 705/19 and 705/20 provide rights re termination and nonrenewal
No waiver of claims under state franchise law
General release not required as condition of renewal/transfer/sale (COMAR 02.02.08.16L)
Franchise fees must be deferred until franchisor has satisfied all material obligations
Jurisdiction/venue provisions requiring litigation outside MD may not be enforceable
Limitations of claims must be at least 3 years from grant of franchise
No waiver of claims under state franchise law
90 days notice of termination with 60 days to cure (Minn. Stat. 80C.14)
180 days notice of non-renewal
Transfer consent must not be unreasonably withheld
Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota
Cannot require jury trial waiver
Cannot require liquidated damages, termination penalties, or judgment notes
Cannot require general release (Minn. Rule 2860.4400(D))
Franchisee cannot consent to franchisor obtaining injunctive relief (but franchisor may seek it)
Indemnify franchisee for trademark use per Minn. Stat. 80C
Registration does not constitute state recommendation
Franchisor may negotiate but cannot use negotiation to impose less favorable terms
Termination in good faith per NY GBL Article 33
No waiver of claims under state franchise law
Covenants not to compete may be unenforceable under ND law (NDCC 9-08-06)
General release as condition of renewal/transfer may be unenforceable
Consent to out-of-state jurisdiction or arbitration may not be enforceable
NH choice of law may not be enforceable
Waiver of punitive damages may not be enforceable
Waiver of jury trial may not be enforceable
Limitation of claims may not be enforceable
Provisions requiring franchisee to pay franchisor's costs in enforcement action may not be enforceable
RI Franchise Investment Act (19-28.1-14) provides for profit-sharing arrangements, noncompete, and transfer provisions
No statement or acknowledgment shall disclaim reliance on representations in FDD
Virginia franchise law applies to certain provisions
Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law (Wis. Stat. Ch. 135) supersedes inconsistent FA provisions
Prohibits termination/cancellation/non-renewal/substantial change without good cause
90 days prior written notice required
60 days to cure deficiency; if cured, notice is void
